title: Mouthwatering Ethiopian Water Chestnut Stew
description: A tangy, dense, festive stew that highlights crisp water chestnuts, bright berbere spice, and a splash of lemon for a low-calorie, nutrient-rich meal.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic, grated ginger, and berbere spice, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in the crushed tomatoes and vegetable broth, bringing the mixture to a gentle boil.
Add the sliced water chestnuts, reduce heat, and simmer uncovered for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the lemon juice and season with salt to taste.
Remove from heat, let the stew rest for 5 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh cilantro.
Enjoy the bright, tangy flavors of this low-calorie Ethiopian stew.
